text: Git branching is an essential part of version control. When working on a project, developers create feature branches to build new features or fix bugs without affecting the main codebase. Each branch is an independent line of development. Once the work is complete, the branch is merged back into the main branch. This is often done through a pull request, where team members review the code before merging. This workflow keeps the main branch stable and allows multiple tasks to be developed simultaneously. Understanding how to create, manage, and merge branches is key to efficient collaboration in modern software development.
